title: Implement epub folder scanning backend
description: Implement the Rust backend command that scans the configured local epub folder and returns a list of epub files (title, author, filename, size, last-modified). Expose as a Tauri command callable from the frontend. This list feeds the epub diff and multi-select UI.

title: Implement safe USB eject backend
description: Implement the Rust backend command to safely eject the eReader USB device on Windows. Use DeviceIoControl or the Windows 'Eject' API to ensure no data loss. Return success/failure to the frontend so the UI can confirm ejection or show an error.
close_reason: Safe eject implemented via DeviceIoControl: lock volume, dismount, eject media. User-friendly error on lock failure.

title: Write unit tests for epub diff logic
description: Write unit tests for the epub diff backend logic. Cover: all files new (empty eReader), no new files (already synced), partial overlap (some new some existing), filename edge cases. Tests should run on Linux in CI without a real eReader device.

title: Build first-run config wizard UI
description: Build the frontend UI that appears when epubl is launched for the first time (no config file found). The wizard should prompt the user to select their local epub folder and confirm or override the detected eReader path. On completion, write the config via the config module and transition to the main app UI. Must be simple enough for non-technical users.

title: Show update available notification banner
description: Build the frontend UI component that displays a non-intrusive banner or badge when the update check finds a new version. The banner should show the new version number and a 'Download update' button that opens the GitHub Releases page in the browser (or triggers in-app update install via tauri-plugin-updater). Dismissible for the session.
close_reason: Dismissible banner implemented in App.svelte: listens to update-available event, shows version + GitHub releases link, dismiss button.

title: Implement config read/write module
description: Implement the Rust backend module that reads and writes the epubl config file. Use the schema defined in the design issue. Config file should live in a standard user data directory (e.g., %APPDATA%/epubl). Expose Tauri commands so the frontend can get/set config values. Handle missing/corrupted config gracefully (first-run defaults).
close_reason: Implemented in src-tauri/src/config.rs: Config struct with TOML read/write, atomic saves, app_config_dir path, Tauri commands get_config/set_config.

title: Build file transfer progress bar UI
description: Build the frontend UI that shows real-time progress during epub file copy. Display a progress bar, current file name, and bytes transferred. Subscribe to the file copy progress events from the backend. Show a completion summary (files copied, any errors) when the transfer finishes. Disable copy button during transfer.

title: Implement USB eReader detection backend
description: Implement the Rust backend logic that detects when a USB eReader is connected to Windows. Use WMI or winapi to enumerate removable drives, identify eReaders by device type/name, and emit events to the frontend when a device connects or disconnects. This is the foundation for all eReader interaction features.
close_reason: USB detection backend implemented in src-tauri/src/usb.rs. WMI event subscriptions, VID-based identification, drive letter resolution, Tauri events. Stubs for non-Windows.

title: Implement update check on app startup
description: On app startup, call tauri-plugin-updater to check for a new version against the GitHub Releases update manifest. If an update is available, emit an event to the frontend. Do not block app startup or auto-install without user confirmation. Cache the check result to avoid repeated network calls within a session.
close_reason: check_for_update spawned on startup, emits update-available event with version+notes. All errors non-fatal.

title: Set up GitHub Releases distribution workflow
description: Configure the CI pipeline to automatically publish a GitHub Release when a version tag is pushed. The release should include the Windows installer artifact and the update manifest JSON required by tauri-plugin-updater. This is the distribution mechanism that lets the developer push updates from Linux to Windows users.

title: Implement epub diff against eReader contents
description: Implement the Rust backend command that compares the local epub folder against the eReader's epub directory and returns which files are new (on local but not on eReader), which are already synced, and which may be outdated. Expose as a Tauri command. This is the core logic that drives the sync workflow.

title: Integration smoke test for file transfer
description: Write an integration smoke test that exercises the full file copy flow end-to-end: mock a source epub folder, mock an eReader mount path (temp directory), call the file copy command, verify files arrive correctly, verify progress events were emitted. Run in CI on Linux using temp directories to simulate the eReader.

title: Build eReader detection status UI section
description: Build the frontend UI component that shows whether an eReader is currently connected. Display device name and mount path when connected; show a friendly 'Connect your eReader' message when not. Subscribe to USB detection backend events to update in real time without polling.
close_reason: Device panel now shows model/drive/Connected badge on connect, friendly message when disconnected. Real-time via Tauri events.

title: Wire Eject button to safe eject backend
description: Add an 'Eject eReader' button to the main UI and wire it to the safe USB eject backend command. Show a confirmation dialog before ejecting. Display success message or error after the call returns. Disable the button when no eReader is detected.

title: Scaffold Tauri project for epubl
description: Initialize the Tauri project skeleton for epubl. Run 'cargo tauri init', set up the frontend (minimal HTML/JS/CSS or a lightweight framework like Solid.js), configure tauri.conf.json with correct app name/identifier/window settings, and verify the app builds and opens a window on Windows (via cross-compilation or CI). This is the foundation all other features build on.
close_reason: Tauri v2 + Svelte v5 project scaffolded. All module stubs in place, lib.rs wires USB commands and watcher.

title: Research auto-update mechanism for Tauri app
description: Investigate tauri-plugin-updater: how it fetches update manifests, how GitHub Releases can serve as the update endpoint, what the update.json manifest must contain, and how to sign updates. Document the findings so the 'update check on startup' feature can be implemented correctly.
notes:
## Auto-update research findings (from tech stack research)

### tauri-plugin-updater (Tauri v2)
- Add to Cargo.toml: tauri-plugin-updater
- Configure in tauri.conf.json: plugins.updater.endpoints and plugins.updater.pubkey
- Checks manifest on startup; if newer version found, emits event to frontend
- Update manifest (latest.json) uploaded as GitHub Release asset
- Tauri updater follows GH CDN redirects correctly

### Keypair setup
  cargo tauri signer generate -w ~/.tauri/epubl.key
  Private key -> GH Actions secret: TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY
  Public key -> tauri.conf.json plugins.updater.pubkey

### Manifest format
{ version, notes, pub_date, platforms: { windows-x86_64: { signature, url } } }

### CI step (in release.yml)
After building, sign the artifact:
  cargo tauri signer sign -k $TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY epubl_x.y.z_x64-setup.exe
Upload latest.json + signed installer to GH Release.

### Signing is mandatory — cannot be disabled in production builds.
close_reason: Research covered in tech stack investigation. Findings documented in notes and docs/architecture.md.

title: Implement file copy with progress events
description: Implement the Rust backend command that copies selected epub files from the local folder to the eReader. Emit progress events (bytes copied, total bytes, current file) to the frontend during the transfer. Handle errors gracefully (disk full, device removed mid-copy). This is the core file transfer primitive.

title: Select tech stack for epubl Windows GUI app
description: Decide on the technology stack for building epubl. Recommendation is Tauri (Rust backend + WebView2 frontend) because it cross-compiles Linux→Windows well, produces small single .exe or NSIS installer, has built-in tauri-plugin-updater for auto-updates, and Rust has wmi/winapi crates for USB detection/eject. Evaluate alternatives and make a final decision so all subsequent work can proceed.
notes:
## Decision: Tauri v2 + Svelte + GitHub Actions CI

### Stack
- Framework: Tauri v2
- Frontend: Svelte v5 + Vite
- CI/Release: GitHub Actions windows-latest (only supported cross-build path)
- Auto-updates: tauri-plugin-updater + static latest.json in GH Releases
- Installer: NSIS, perUser install mode (no admin elevation, installs to %LOCALAPPDATA%)
- USB detection: wmi crate, Win32_DiskDrive queries
- Safe eject: windows-rs, DeviceIoControl

### Why Tauri wins
- 3-5 MB NSIS installer (uses system WebView2, not bundled browser; Electron = 70-150 MB)
- tauri-plugin-updater ready-made; building equivalent in egui requires significant custom work
- HTML/CSS UI trivial for 3-panel layout vs significant custom drawing in egui/iced
- Svelte v5 compiled-away framework = minimal bundle; clean reactivity for 3-panel state

### Cross-compilation
- DO NOT attempt local Linux->Windows cross-compilation for the installer
- GitHub Actions windows-latest is the only supported path
- All WMI/Win32 code behind #[cfg(target_os = "windows")] guards; provide stub impls on Linux

### Updater signing (mandatory)
- cargo tauri signer generate -w ~/.tauri/epubl.key
- Private key in GH Actions secret: TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY
- Public key in tauri.conf.json plugins.updater.pubkey
- Upload latest.json manifest as release asset; set plugins.updater.endpoints to raw URL

### Update manifest format
{ "version": "1.2.0", "notes": "...", "pub_date": "2025-06-01T00:00:00Z",
  "platforms": { "windows-x86_64": { "signature": "<minisign-sig>",
  "url": "https://github.com/vbonduro/epubl/releases/download/v1.2.0/epubl_1.2.0_x64-setup.exe" } } }

### Key gotchas
1. WMI queries must run in spawn_blocking — COM is thread-affine
2. Volume locking before eject fails if files are open — handle gracefully in UI
3. WebView2 pre-installed on Win10 1803+ and all Win11 — include bootstrapper for edge cases
4. NSIS installMode: perUser — no admin prompt, installs to %LOCALAPPDATA%
5. latest.json served as static GH release asset — Tauri updater follows CDN redirects correctly
close_reason: Decision made: Tauri v2 + Svelte v5 + GitHub Actions CI. Captured in docs/architecture.md.

title: Add clickable bookstore URL link to main UI
description: Add a clickable hyperlink in the main app UI that opens the user's preferred ebook store in their default browser. The URL should come from the app config (set during first-run or settings). Use Tauri's shell open API. This gives non-technical users a one-click path to buy new books.

title: Build epub multi-select list UI
description: Build the frontend UI that displays the epub diff results as a selectable list. Show new/unsynced books highlighted; allow the user to check/uncheck individual titles. Include a 'Select All New' shortcut. The selected list feeds the file copy command. Must be clear and usable for non-technical users.

title: Set up CI build pipeline for Windows cross-compilation
description: Configure GitHub Actions (or equivalent CI) to cross-compile the Tauri app from Linux to Windows. Set up the workflow to build on push/PR, produce a Windows .exe artifact, and eventually trigger releases. This enables the developer to ship updates to Windows users from a Linux workstation.
close_reason: CI workflows created: ci.yml (cargo check/clippy/test + frontend build on every push), release.yml (full NSIS build + sign + GH Release on v* tags via tauri-apps/tauri-action). RELEASE.md documents keypair setup and release process.

title: Write unit tests for config module
description: Write unit tests for the config read/write module. Cover: reading a valid config, reading a missing config (first-run defaults), reading a corrupted config (graceful error), writing and re-reading round-trips correctly. Tests should run on Linux in CI without requiring a real Windows environment.

title: Design config schema for epubl
description: Define the configuration schema that epubl will persist between sessions. Config must capture: path to local epub folder, path to eReader mount point (or how to auto-detect it), and any user preferences. Produce a documented schema (e.g., TOML or JSON struct) that the config read/write implementation can follow.
close_reason: Config schema designed and documented in config.rs. Fields: epub_folder, ereader_path (Option), bookstore_url, first_run.

title: Configure single-file Windows installer packaging
description: Configure Tauri to produce a single-file NSIS installer (.exe) for Windows distribution. Set up installer metadata (app name, version, icon, publisher). Ensure the installer creates Start Menu shortcuts and an uninstaller entry. Test that the installer runs cleanly on a fresh Windows environment.

title: Research USB eReader detection on Windows
description: Research how to detect when a USB eReader device is connected on Windows. Investigate WMI queries, Win32_DiskDrive/Win32_LogicalDisk, USB device arrival events, and Rust crates (wmi, winapi) that expose these APIs. Document findings so the implementation issue (USB detection feature) can proceed with a clear approach.
notes:
## USB eReader Detection Research Findings

### Recommended approach: hybrid CM_Register_Notification + WMI

1. CM_Register_Notification (windows-rs crate) for truly event-driven arrival/removal detection
   - No window required, no polling, Windows 8+ (safe assumption)
   - Use GUID_DEVINTERFACE_VOLUME = {53F5630D-B6BF-11D0-94F2-00A0C91EFB8B}
   - Callback fires on CM_NOTIFY_ACTION_DEVICEINTERFACEARRIVAL / DEVICEINTERFACEREMOVAL
2. WMI one-shot query (wmi crate) after each arrival to get device identity
   - Win32_DiskDrive -> Win32_DiskDriveToDiskPartition -> Win32_LogicalDiskToPartition -> Win32_LogicalDisk
   - Check PNPDeviceID for known VIDs; check Model substring as fallback

### Known eReader USB Vendor IDs (in PNPDeviceID as VID_xxxx)
- 0x1949 = Amazon/Kindle (all Paperwhite, Oasis, Scribe, etc.)
- 0x2080 = Rakuten Kobo (Clara, Libra, Sage, Elipsa, etc.)
- 0x0525 / 0x2899 = PocketBook
- 0xFDE8 = Bookeen Cybook
- 0x2207 = Onyx Boox (Rockchip VID, shared with generic Android — use Model name too)

### Model name fallback matching
- Model.to_uppercase().contains("KINDLE")
- Model.to_uppercase().contains("KOBO")
- Model.to_uppercase().contains("POCKETBOOK")
- Model.to_uppercase().contains("BOOKEEN")

### Drive letter extraction (from WM_DEVICECHANGE, if used instead)
- DBT_DEVTYP_VOLUME: dbcv_unitmask bitmask, bit N = drive letter 'A'+N
- Note: USB eReaders do NOT set DBTF_MEDIA flag (that's CD/DVD only)

### Crate recommendations
[target.'cfg(target_os = "windows")'.dependencies]
wmi = "0.14"
windows = { version = "0.58", features = [
    "Win32_Devices_DeviceAndDriverInstallation",
    "Win32_Storage_FileSystem",
    "Win32_System_Wmi",
] }

### Simpler fallback (if CM_Register_Notification proves complex)
WMI __InstanceCreationEvent WITHIN 2 for Win32_DiskDrive — introduces up to 2s latency
but is entirely within the wmi crate ecosystem, no unsafe code needed.
For an epub sync app this latency is acceptable.

### Tauri integration
Spawn background tokio task from Tauri setup hook.
Emit Tauri events: ereader-connected { drive_letter, model, vendor } and ereader-disconnected.
WMI COM calls must be in spawn_blocking (COM is thread-affine).
close_reason: Research complete. Approach: CM_Register_Notification + WMI one-shot query. VIDs and crates documented in notes and docs/architecture.md.
